creator: felipe@neuwald.biz
text:
I have one Dell PowerEdge SC1435 Server. I already run FreeBSD 6.2 and
6.3 on that server (and everything works fine), but, when I try to install
FreeBSD 7.0 (i386 or amd64), I can't install (got HDD error). Already
changed media disc and tryed to install via FTP. When copying files (doc
distribution), I got HDD erros. If I don't install doc distribution, got
HDD errors on other packages. Sometimes, I can install minimum distribution
set (and sometimes I got the same HDD errors). Already changed HDD. The
guys from Dell came here and changed motherboard, network card, and
performed bios and devices upgrade. Got the same errors. Now, the server
is running (minimal distribution installed). I don't thinkg it's hardware
problem cause the server runs ok with FreeBSD 6.2/6.3 and I already
performed all hardware diagnostics from Dell.

When I try to start sshd, I got:

testhost# /etc/rc.d/./sshd start
/libexec/ld-elf.so.1: /lib/libcrypto.so.5: Unsupported relocation type 171060785
 in non-PLT relocations

/libexec/ld-elf.so.1: /lib/libcrypto.so.5: Unsupported relocation type 171060785
 in non-PLT relocations

/libexec/ld-elf.so.1: /lib/libcrypto.so.5: Unsupported relocation type 171060785
 in non-PLT relocations

I started telnetd on that server. When try to connect via telnet, got:

[root@r01 /usr/home/felipe]# telnet 189.xx.xx.x
Trying 189.xx.xx.x...
Connected to 189.xx.xx.x.
Escape character is '^]'.
/libexec/ld-elf.so.1: /lib/libcrypto.so.5: Unsupported relocation type 171060785 in non-PLT relocations

Connection closed by foreign host.

Here is the dmesg result: http://www.neuwald.biz/files/fbsd7.0-dell_poweredge_sc1435-dmesg.txt

How-To-Repeat: Try to install FreeBSD 7.0 (i386 or amd64) on a Dell PowerEdge SC 1435 machine.
